name: I’m constantly coughing and wheezing at my factory job; what are my first steps?

mainEntity.name: I’m constantly coughing and wheezing at my factory job; what are my first steps?

acceptedAnswer.text: Seek medical care immediately and notify your employer in writing about your symptoms and their connection to work. Document the specific fumes or dust you are exposed to and request Safety Data Sheets (SDS) from your manager to provide to your doctor.
acceptedAnswer.description: If you suspect your lung issues are work-related, the first step is seeking medical attention and specifically mentioning your work environment to the doctor. In Utah and Idaho, you must notify your employer in writing as soon as possible—typically within 180 days in Utah or 60 days in Idaho—to preserve your right to benefits. Delaying this report is the most common reason claims are denied, as insurers will argue the illness happened elsewhere. You should also request a copy of the Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for any chemicals you work around. Documentation of your symptoms and the specific dates of exposure will be critical for your claim. Professional legal guidance is often necessary to link toxic exposure to specific respiratory diagnoses.
